analysis: That opening night — 5 hours 24 minutes with two and a half hours just lying there awake — was rough, but look at what you did after it: you strung together five nights in a row above 7 hours and then closed the week with back-to-back 8-plus-hour nights, including last night's 8 hours 39 minutes with a full two hours of REM, which is genuinely beautiful. The wind-down stack is clearly doing something real. The one thing worth noticing is that June 23rd snuck in nearly two hours and 45 minutes of wakefulness even on a decent total, so the sleep is still fragile in the middle of the night on some nights — your body is getting the hours but occasionally fighting to keep them. You're trending in exactly the right direction, though, and two hours of REM on a Sunday night is the kind of thing that makes the whole week feel different in your body.
